history.content:
<thought>
I am at row 07 column D, facing upward. The cell directly in front of me at row 06 column D is lava, so moving forward would be deadly. The cell to my right at row 07 column E is free floor, so the safest next step is to turn right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front of me at row 06 column D is safe floor, while the cells to my left side are lava corridors and to my right is a wall. Moving forward is safe and continues along the open path.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward, with the cell directly in front of me at row 06 column D. That cell is a safe floor tile. The cells to my left are lava and the cells to my right are walls, so moving forward is the safest and only useful progress.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front of me at row 06 column D is a safe floor cell, and continuing upward follows a clear vertical corridor. The goal is not yet visible, but moving forward is safe and better than turning toward walls or lava.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward, so the cell directly in front of me is row 06 column D, which is a safe floor tile. The cells to my left include lava at row 07 column C, and there is a wall immediately to my right at row 07 column E. Moving forward is the safest progress into the open corridor.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front of me at row 06 column D is a safe floor tile. The cells to my right are blocked by a wall, and the left side includes lava nearby. Moving forward is the safest progress and leads into the open passage.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front of me is row 06 column D, which is a safe floor tile. To my right is a wall at row 07 column E, and to my left is lava at row 07 column C. Moving forward is the only safe advancing move.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front of me is row 06 column D, which is a wall, so moving forward is not possible. The open floor is to my left at row 07 column C, while the cell to my right is a wall. I should turn left to face the safe open path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front of me is row 06 column D, which is a safe floor tile. The goal is visible at row 01 column D, straight ahead in the same column, and the path upward through column D is clear of lava and walls from rows 06 to 01. So the best next step is to move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible ahead-left relative to my current position. The cell directly in front of me is safe floor, while turning would not immediately improve my path. Moving forward advances toward the open corridor and keeps me off the lava.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible ahead-left of my position. I am at row 07 column D facing upward, so the cell directly in front of me is row 06 column D, which is a safe floor cell. Moving forward advances me toward the corridor leading to the goal, while turning is unnecessary. I should avoid the lava on the left side and the wall on the right.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward, with the cell directly in front at row 06 column D being a safe floor tile. The goal is visible further ahead at row 04 column D, and the straight path toward it is clear through row 06 column D and row 05 column D. Turning is unnecessary, and moving forward advances directly toward the goal while avoiding lava and walls.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is directly ahead in the same column, but there is a safe floor cell between me and the goal. The cell immediately in front of me is safe, while the cells to the left include lava and the right is blocked by a wall. Moving forward is the best safe step toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is directly in front of me at row 06 column D, one cell ahead of my current position at row 07 column D. Moving forward reaches the goal immediately. The lava cells to the left are irrelevant since I do not need to step on them.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
